research.title: Drone Designer — Intelligent Dynamics Control Lab
research.organization: University of Calgary · WISE Program · Intelligent Dynamics Control Lab
research.summary: As part of the WiseUCalgary research program at the University of Calgary, I developed a real-time crop monitoring drone system integrating an esp8266, DHT11, Photoresistor and HC-SRO4, to track field conditions and data remotely, optimizing crop health and water usage. Collaborating with a multidisciplinary team, I gained hands-on experience in sensor integration and data analysis, contributing to a low-cost solution that enhances agricultural efficiency while minimizing fieldwork. This opportunity deepened my understanding of the intersection between technology and sustainable farming practices.
research.outcomes: WISE STEM Award; presented at WISE Research Symposium.

research.title: Researcher — Vascular Training Platform
research.summary: I gained hands-on expertise in advanced neuroimaging techniques, including MRI scans and Transcranial Doppler Ultrasound, to assess and analyze cerebral health. Collaborating with leading experts in the field allowed me to deepen my knowledge of neuroimaging applications and vascular health. Additionally, I engaged in cognitive assessments, gaining insights into how lifestyle factors can influence dementia prevention. This experience has equipped me with the skills and knowledge necessary to contribute to future innovations in medical science.
research.contribution: Applied neuroimaging (MRI, Transcranial Doppler Ultrasound) and cognitive assessments related to vascular disease and dementia prevention.
research.outcomes: Hands-on neuroimaging expertise; foundation for future medical science innovation.

research.title: Solving the Microplastic Crisis — Crustex
research.summary: Hackathon project where my group and I developed Crustex, a sustainable solution to the rising numbers of microplastics found in synthetic clothing. It uses crustacean shells, that are typically thrown away, to create a biodegradable textile that is both sustainable and effective at reducing microplastic shedding from clothing. Chitosan is extracted from the shells and used to create the textile.
research.outcomes: Most Innovative Idea Award from TKS.
